articles.title: A ‘pound of flesh’ from data centers: one senator’s answer to AI job losses
articles.summary: Fears of AI-driven job loss are growing fast, and they’re fueling backlash against data centers. Sen. Mark Warner suggests taxing them to help workers survive the transition.

articles.title: Mistral releases a new open-source model for speech generation
articles.summary: Mistral's new speech model can run on a smartwatch or a smartphone.

articles.title: A little-known Croatian startup is coming for the robotaxi market with help from Uber
articles.summary: Verne, the robotaxi company launched under Rimac Group, is starting with a service in Zagreb, Croatia.

articles.title: The least surprising chapter of the Manus story is what’s happening right now
articles.summary: Did anyone think there would not be a reckoning over this tie-up?

articles.title: Mercor competitor Deccan AI raises $25M, sources experts from India
articles.summary: Deccan AI concentrates its workforce in India to manage quality in a fast-growing but fragmented AI training market.

articles.title: Delve did the security compliance on LiteLLM, an AI project hit by malware
articles.summary: LiteLLM offers an AI open source project used by millions that was infected by credential harvesting malware.

articles.title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
articles.summary: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

articles.title: Convicted spyware chief hints that Greece’s government was behind dozens of phone hacks
articles.summary: The spyware founder's comments are the most direct suggestion yet from anyone inside Intellexa that the Mitsotakis government authorized the hacking of dozens of phones belonging to senior Greek government ministers, opposition leaders, military officials, and journalists.

articles.title: Who’s driving Waymo’s self-driving cars? Sometimes, the police.
articles.summary: First responders have had to take control of Waymo vehicles and move them during emergency situations, including in at least two active crime scenes, TechCrunch found.

articles.title: Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it ‘Pied Piper’
articles.summary: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.

articles.title: The snow gods: How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather app
articles.summary: The best snow-forecasting app for skiers and snowboarders isn’t from any of the federally funded weather services. Nor from any of the big-name brands.
